summary: A school management web app with student and parent registration/linking flow and a parent dashboard featuring a real-time live chat system built with Firebase.
architectural_notes: Use Firebase Authentication for user identity in chat
Use Firebase Realtime Database or Firestore with real-time listeners for chat messages
Apply secure Firebase rules to protect chat data
Add basic error handling for network disconnection in chat

new_feature_requests.summary: Parent dashboard: real-time live chat using Firebase Realtime Database/Firestore with WebSocket-like instant messaging (no page refresh)
Live chat: show online/offline status of users
Live chat: typing indicator ('User is typing...')
Live chat: show message timestamps and auto-scroll to latest message
Live chat: store all messages in cloud database (Firestore/Firebase Realtime DB)
Live chat: push notifications when a new message arrives
Live chat: mobile responsive UI with clean chat bubbles, optimized for PWA

Replace the existing localStorage-based chat in ParentDashboard.tsx and StudentMessagesPage.tsx with a polling-free real-time chat system. Use the Internet Computer backend (Motoko) to store messages in stable state with a timestamp, senderId, senderRole (parent/student), messageText, and a read flag. Expose query and update functions: sendMessage, getMessages, and markMessagesRead.

Implement a React Query polling loop (1-second interval) in both ParentDashboard.tsx and StudentMessagesPage.tsx to simulate real-time message delivery by refetching getMessages frequently, giving the appearance of instant updates without a page refresh.

Add online/offline presence tracking. Store a lastSeen timestamp and isOnline boolean per user in Motoko stable state. Expose updatePresence and getPresence functions. Call updatePresence on a regular interval (every 30 seconds) from the frontend. Display a green dot for online (seen within 60 seconds) and a grey dot with 'Last seen' time for offline users in the chat header.

Add a typing indicator. Store a isTyping boolean and typingUserId in component state. When the local user types in the input field, broadcast a setTyping(true) flag via a Motoko update call and clear it after 3 seconds of inactivity. Poll getTypingStatus on a 1-second interval and display 'User is typing...' with an animated ellipsis below the message list when the remote party is typing.

Display a formatted timestamp (e.g., '10:32 AM') beneath each chat bubble. Group messages by date with a centered date separator (e.g., 'Today', 'Yesterday', 'Dec 12'). Auto-scroll the message list to the latest message whenever new messages arrive or a message is sent.

Redesign the chat UI in ParentDashboard.tsx and StudentMessagesPage.tsx with modern chat bubbles: outgoing messages aligned right with a primary colour background, incoming messages aligned left with a neutral background, rounded corners, sender avatar initials, and a clean input bar with a send button. Ensure the layout is fully mobile responsive with the message list taking available vertical space and the input bar pinned to the bottom.

Show an in-app notification toast (using the existing toast provider) when a new message arrives while the user is on a different page or the chat panel is not in focus. Use the existing browser Notification API (already configured in the service worker) to send a push notification with the sender's name and message preview when the document is hidden.

Add basic network error handling in the chat components. Show a non-blocking banner ('Connection lost – retrying…') when backend calls fail due to network issues. Automatically retry failed message sends up to 3 times with exponential backoff. Display a persistent error state if all retries fail, with a manual 'Retry' button.

Update the unread message badge in Layout.tsx to reflect the real unread message count fetched from the Motoko backend via the getMessages and markMessagesRead functions, replacing the current localStorage-based unread count logic.
